label: Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields

node.label: Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields
node.definition: An invertible or continuous-time transformation transports samples between distributions.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields is modeled as depending on Flow matching & transport objectives, Differential equations and feeding downstream nodes such as Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models.
examples: atlas downstream link to Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models
combined with Flow matching & transport objectives
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Flow matching & transport objectives, Differential equations.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields has a `uses` dependency on Flow matching & transport objectives because Flow matching & transport objectives is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields has a `uses` dependency on Differential equations because Differential equations is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields has a `composes` dependency on Reparameterization & differentiable sampling with `common` strength.
